title: Position Reconstruction & Mark-to-Market
notes: Reference is portable across sqlite/duckdb/postgres/mysql. mssql deviation: natural log is LOG(x) in T-SQL, so replace LN(...) with LOG(...) (single-token change) -- mssql omitted from the engines list on that basis. naive/2_no_dedup matches the reference on the mild visible sample (no injected duplicate fills or double-posted snapshots) and fails only on the hidden black fixture; naive/1 (splits) and naive/3 (month-end) already diverge on the visible sample. Split-factor product uses EXP(SUM(LN(mult))); month-end reporting dates are the real month-end sessions on which positions snapshots land (portable, no calendar arithmetic).
